tool.name: dada2: mergeSequenceTable
tool.description: merge 2 or more sequence table together

configfiles.configfile:
@READ_FOO@ @WRITE_FOO@
library(dada2, quietly=T)
ltables <- list() #for $t in $tables: ltables[["$t.element_identifier"]] <- t(read.table('$t', header=T, sep="\t", row.names=1, check.names=FALSE)) #end for
seqtab <- mergeSequenceTables(tables = ltables, repeats = "$repeats", orderBy = "$orderBy", "$tryRC")
write.data( seqtab, '$merged', "dada2_sequencetable" )

param.label: Select how to handle duplicated sample names between different tables
param.option: Stop the tool when a sample name appear twice
Add sample names together when they appear multiple times

param.label: Select how the table should be sorted
merge reverse- complemented together
param.help: sequences whose reverse complement matches an earlier sequence will be reverse- complemented and merged together with that earlier sequence

has_text.text: Duplicated sample names detected in the sequence table row names

tool.help:
Description ...........
This function merge at least 2 sequence table together
The sequence table is a matrix with rows corresponding to (and named by) the samples, and columns corresponding to (and named by) the sequence variants.
Usage .....
**Input**: The result of dada2_makeSequenceTable.
**Output**: A data set of type dada2_sequencetable, i.e. a tabular with a row for each sample, and a column for each unique sequence across all the samples. The columns are named by the sequence.
.......
@HELP_OVERVIEW@
